title: dysoxylum grande
intro: Dysoxylum grande, also known as the Philippine peruviana, is a species of tree in the family Meliaceae, native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Dysoxylum grande include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's bark and roots are commonly used to make infusions and decoctions that can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to affected areas.
